# MMRcaseselection ![](reference/figures/MMRhex.png)

[![CRAN
status](http://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/MMRcaseselection)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=MMRcaseselection)
[![Project Status: Active – The project has reached a stable, usable
state and is being actively
developed.](https://www.repostatus.org/badges/latest/active.svg)](https://www.repostatus.org/#active)
[![GPLv3
License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-GPL%20v3-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/license)
![](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/grand-total/MMRcaseselection)

## Overview

Classification and choice of cases for case studies based on regression
results.

For now, the package contains functions for the classification of cases
as typical, deviant, extreme or pathway cases and the choice of these
types of cases. At this stage, the focus is on single cases and case
selection based on linear regression models (class `lm`).

The package is [available on
CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=MMRcaseselection). The version
on CRAN Github is 0.2.0. The package can be installed from CRAN with the
following command.

``` r

install.packages("MMRcaseselection")
```

The package can be installed from Github with the following command.

``` r

pak::pak("ingorohlfing/MMRcaseselection")
```

The documentation of the package and examples can be found
[here](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/docs/). Please
open an issue on Github if problems come up.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Work on the package was funded by the European Research Council (ERC)
under the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation program
(grant agreement number 638425, *Enhanced Qualitative and Multimethod
Research*).

# Package index

## Classification of cases

Functions for designating cases as belonging to a specific type

- [`extreme_on_x()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/extreme_on_x.md)
  : Extremeness of cases on an independent variable
- [`extreme_on_y()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/extreme_on_y.md)
  : Extremeness of cases on the dependent variable
- [`pathway()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/pathway.md)
  : Pathway case
- [`predint()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/predint.md)
  : Classification of cases as typical and deviant using a prediction
  interval.
- [`residstd()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/residstd.md)
  : Classification of cases as typical and deviant using the standard
  deviation of the residuals.

## Selection of cases

Functions for choosing cases belonging to a specific type

- [`extreme_on_x()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/extreme_on_x.md)
  : Extremeness of cases on an independent variable
- [`extreme_on_y()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/extreme_on_y.md)
  : Extremeness of cases on the dependent variable
- [`most_deviant()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/most_deviant.md)
  : Identification of the most deviant case
- [`most_overpredicted()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/most_overpredicted.md)
  : Identification of the most overpredicted case
- [`most_typical()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/most_typical.md)
  : Identification of the most typical case
- [`most_underpredicted()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/most_underpredicted.md)
  : Identification of the most underpredicted case
- [`pathway()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/pathway.md)
  : Pathway case
- [`predint()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/predint.md)
  : Classification of cases as typical and deviant using a prediction
  interval.

## Visualization

Functions for visualizing case classifications

- [`pathway_xvr()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/pathway_xvr.md)
  : Plot of residuals against pathway variable
- [`predint_plot()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/predint_plot.md)
  : Plot of typical and deviant cases with prediction intervals
- [`residstd_plot()`](https://ingorohlfing.github.io/MMRcaseselection/reference/residstd_plot.md)
  : Plot of typical and deviant cases based on residuals' standard
  deviation
